{{Quote|Sorry to rush you, but I don't trust Ferret. That sand-slug would sell the goods out from under us if we were even a ''minute'' late for our appointment.|Luke Skywalker to Lando Calrissian|<ref name="marvel59" />}}
'''Orion Ferret''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] weapons trader and gunrunner who owned and operated the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] ''[[Bazarre]]'', which served as a large black market hub during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Known for his unscrupulous business practices, Ferret used a pet [[watchbeast]] called [[Ceasar]] to protect his wares on the junk [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Patch-4]]. Ferret once attempted to betray and [[Death/Legends|kill]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leaders [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] over a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] sale, only for his plot to be exposed and foiled.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Mother said there'd be days like this...|Orion Ferret, as Chewbacca prepares to rip his head off|<ref name="marvel59" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Orion Ferret was the owner and proprietor of ''[[Bazarre]]'', a massive [[Space station/Legends|space station]] that served as a widely trafficked hub for merchants during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Ferret himself was a noted gunrunner and weapons dealer, and ran a station where almost anything could be bought and sold. He also made use of the nearby garbage [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Patch-4]], using a dangerous [[watchbeast]] named [[Ceasar]] to protect the assets he hid there, while terrorizing the people who lived upon it. During the height of the Galactic Civil War, Ferret made arrangements to sell four black market [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. The deal was brokered with two Rebel leaders—[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], the latter a longtime [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and rogue who had past dealings with Ferret—who met with Ferret aboard ''Bazarre'' and negotiated the sale.<ref name="marvel59" />

After receiving his payment, Ferret told the Rebels that the TIE fighters were hidden in the wastelands of Patch-4. He gave them the proper coordinates and allowed them the use of one of his [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] to pick them up. Lando, who distrusted him, left the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] behind on ''Bazarre'' to keep a watch on Ferret. Calrissian had good cause to be suspicious—Ferret had begun to plan their deaths before they arrived, plotting to remotely activate the shuttle's ejection seats and keeping the money, the TIE fighters and Calrissian's ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'' [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. When Chewbacca's presence complicating the plan, Ferret instead pivoted to a backup, relying on Ceasar to kill the Rebels instead. With the aid of his assistant [[P'Lor]], Ferret used a gelatinous [[spritzer]] solution to imprison Chewbacca, who managed to break free.<ref name="marvel59" />

With an enraged Wookiee chasing him through the corridors of ''Bazarre'', Ferret attempted to flee in his personal shuttlecraft, but a few well-aimed shots from Chewbacca's [[Bowcaster/Legends|bowcaster]] rendered the ship unusable. Chewbacca eventually cornered Ferret and threatened to rip the man's head off, only to be stopped by the timely return of Calrissian and Skywalker, who had managed to subdue Ceasar by commandeering the [[sonic pacifier]] Ferret used to control the beast. With their TIEs safely secured, the Rebels called Chewbacca off, allowing Ferret to live.<ref name="marvel59" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I had dealings with Ferret back when I ran Cloud City—and believe me, he's used to dirt!|Lando Calrissian|<ref name="marvel59" />}}
A Human notorious for his untrustworthiness, Orion Ferret garnered a bad reputation for duplicity from almost everyone he did business with. Those qualities, along with his complete lack of regard for everyone he came into contact with—and for the welfare of innocent people—made Ferret into a notorious underworld figure. However, Ferret was a physically weak and unimposing man, more comfortable concocting secret plans than asserting himself in person.<ref name="marvel59" />

==Behind the scenes==
Orion Ferret made his first and only appearance in [[Star Wars (1977) 59|''Star Wars'' 59]], released on [[February 16]], [[1982]],<ref name="tcc">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref> which was written by [[David Michelinie]] and illustrated by [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]], who also did the cover art.<ref name="marvel59" />
